"ATI Radeon Mobility"   (no version numbers)

"ATI Technologies Inc Rage Mobility P/M AGP 2x"

---

### Post by dodgePT on 2007-06-02
You must use one of two methods. Either install fglrx (proprietary driver)+XGL or open source driver+AIGLX.

I can see that you have 'Driver "ati"' in your 'Device' section. To enable direct rendering you must choose one of the above methods and change 'Driver "ati"' to 'Driver "fglrx"' or 'Driver "radeon"'. You got plenty of threads explaining this, make a search :)

---

### Post by tseliot on 2007-06-02
try setting the driver to "radeon" instead of "ati" in the Section Device of your xorg.conf and restart the Xserver

---

### Post by myname on 2007-06-03
Hmmm, just installed a fresh copy of Kubuntu feisty, and then installed envy as to easy the installation of the ATI drivers (Radeon x1600).  However, here is the output of the fglrxinfo:

> 
~$ fglrxinfo |grep direct
Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":0.0".
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect


Here is the section of my xorg.conf

> 
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Generic Video Card"
	Driver	"fglrx"
	Option	"VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	"OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID		"PCI:5:0:0"
EndSection


Not sure why it's not recognizing it as a radeon.

any help?

### Post by dodgePT on 2007-06-05
You can try 'sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg', maybe your card will be detected, or maybe it won't, i experienced that same 'mesa' problem and never got past it (i couldn't get direct rendering). Solved it reinstalling ubuntu and using radeon open source drivers. Never messed with fglrx again :D

---

### Post by dgrafix on 2007-06-06
Still no joy,

Im beginning to give up on 3D openGL with this card as ive tried all sorts. The 2D seems to be accelerated as normal desktop apps are real smooth but I think my problem is its not a radeon but a mach64 chipset and there appears to be no drivers for it. If i use anything other than "ati" it crashes.

Its dual booting windows so i guess for using blender(for very simple models) and google earth i will just have to restart into windoze just for those 2 programs (a real shame). :-( The reason i want to get it working is its a really compact compaq evo n400c laptop about (13" from corner2corner*half inch high) and great for travelling with. Its got a capable P3 @ just under 1ghz so is a real shame i cannot get the accellerated gfx.

Other than direct renderig, it runs 7.04 very very well out of the box as it were.
